en: If your company is required to comply with Czech Republic Social Insurance, you will be asked to provide relevant documents in a subsequent form.
Czech Republic Statutory Labor Insurance Applicability Assessment

The social insurance system in the Czech Republic is administered by the state through the Czech Social Security Administration (ČSSZ), compulsory for all employees (and self-employed persons with some differences), financed by contributions from employers, employees and self-employed persons, and regulated by law (in particular Act No 589/1992 Coll.).
Is your company required to comply with Czech Republic Social Insurance and maintain applicable documentation to show compliance?
Please select from the following reasons your company is exempt from compling with Czech Republic Social Insurance.
The company is composed of employees earning less than CZK 2,000 per month or working less than 14 successive calendar days in a month.

The company has foreign or diplomatic employee(s) working temporarily (up to 24 months) in Czech Republic that are covered by the home country's scheme and are prepared to provide a copy of the A1 certificate(s) upon request.
The company is a sole proprietorship.
